text:
GYNECOLOGIC ONCOLOGY - FOLLOW-UP VISIT
Attending: Gynecologic Oncology

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Routine surveillance visit. Patient presents for scheduled follow-up on niraparib maintenance therapy, approximately 27 months from initial diagnosis and 18 months since initiation of niraparib 150 mg daily.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Ms. Delacroix is a 54-year-old postmenopausal woman with a history of FIGO stage IIIC high-grade serous carcinoma of the ovary (pT3c pN1 M0), harboring a germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (c.5266dupC, 5382insC). She presents today for routine surveillance on niraparib maintenance. She reports no new symptoms. Specifically, she denies new abdominal pain, bloating, or distension. No change in bowel or bladder habits. No unintentional weight loss, early satiety, or constitutional symptoms. No new neurologic complaints. Denies dyspnea, pleuritic chest pain, or cough.

To briefly summarize her oncologic course: she was diagnosed approximately 27 months ago with stage IIIC HGSC after presenting with progressive abdominal distension, pelvic pressure, and a 12-pound weight loss over three months. CA-125 at diagnosis was 1,847 U/mL. She underwent primary debulking surgery with total abdominal hysterectomy, BSO, bilateral pelvic and para-aortic lymphadenectomy, omentectomy, appendectomy, diaphragmatic peritoneal stripping, and excision of multiple peritoneal implants, achieving R0 resection (no macroscopic residual disease). Surgical pathology confirmed HGSC arising from the fallopian tubes (STIC lesions identified bilaterally), with involvement of bilateral ovaries, omentum, pelvic and diaphragmatic peritoneum, liver surface, and pelvic/para-aortic lymph nodes (4/22 pelvic, 2/14 para-aortic positive). Germline and somatic NGS revealed BRCA1 germline pathogenic variant with somatic LOH, TP53 p.R248W, RB1 truncating mutation, CCNE1 amplification, and HRD-positive status (HRD score 56, above threshold). TMB was low at 4.2 mut/Mb.

She completed 6 cycles of carboplatin AUC 6 / paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 (with paclitaxel dose reduced to 135 mg/m2 for cycles 5 and 6 due to Grade 3 peripheral neuropathy with transient left foot drop). End-of-treatment CA-125 normalized to 18 U/mL and PET-CT showed complete metabolic response. She was initiated on niraparib maintenance at an individualized starting dose of 200 mg daily approximately 9.5 months after diagnosis. Three weeks into niraparib, she developed Grade 3 thrombocytopenia (platelet nadir 42,000/uL), requiring hold and subsequent dose reduction to 150 mg daily upon platelet recovery. She has been on niraparib 150 mg daily continuously since approximately month 11 from diagnosis. On this dose she has shown stable Grade 1 thrombocytopenia (platelets in the 105,000-130,000 range) and stable Grade 1 anemia (Hgb ~10.5-11.2 g/dL), without any further Grade 2 or higher cytopenias and without need for further dose reduction. Prior surveillance imaging at 15 and 21 months from diagnosis was negative for disease recurrence.

Today she returns at approximately 27 months post-diagnosis. She feels well. She has returned to full-time work as a high school librarian. She reports good functional status and is engaging in regular walking. She continues venlafaxine 37.5 mg daily for hot flashes with good effect. She uses vaginal lubricants for dryness with adequate relief. She does note very mild tingling in her toes bilaterally, primarily noticeable only after prolonged standing, which she characterizes as not bothersome and substantially improved from prior. Left foot drop has fully resolved. No falls.

Psychosocially, she has been attending an ovarian cancer survivors' support group, which she finds helpful for managing residual anxiety about recurrence. She reports occasional intrusive thoughts about recurrence, particularly around scheduled scans, but these are not impairing daily function. She has been enrolled in a BRCA1 ovarian cancer survivor registry study. Her daughter (age 26) tested positive for the 5382insC variant and is being followed at a high-risk cancer genetics program; this remains emotionally significant for the patient.

Breast surveillance: Annual breast MRI was completed approximately 2 months ago per NCCN guidelines for BRCA1 carriers and was reported as negative. Mammography also current. She has revisited the question of risk-reducing mastectomy and continues to elect surveillance rather than prophylactic surgery at this time. She is fully informed and this preference is documented.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Constitutional: No fever, chills, or night sweats. Stable weight. Good appetite.
Neurologic: Mild residual bilateral toe tingling only with prolonged standing, non-progressive; no new deficits.
Cardiovascular: No chest pain, palpitations, or lower extremity edema.
Respiratory: No dyspnea, cough, or hemoptysis.
Gastrointestinal: No abdominal pain, bloating, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, constipation, or change in stool caliber. No rectal bleeding.
Genitourinary: No hematuria, dysuria, or pelvic pain.
Musculoskeletal: No bone pain.
Skin/Integument: Hair regrowth complete and full. No petechiae, bruising, or mucosal bleeding noted by patient.
Endocrine/Metabolic: Menopausal symptoms (hot flashes, vaginal dryness) managed and stable.
Psychiatric: Mild health-related anxiety, managed via support group. No depression, no suicidal ideation.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
General: Well-appearing, well-nourished woman. In no acute distress. Alert and oriented x3. Normal affect.
Vital signs: Blood pressure 118/74 mmHg, HR 72 bpm, RR 14, Temperature 36.7 C, SpO2 99% on room air. Weight 73 kg, BMI 27.1 kg/m2.
Head/Eyes/Ears/Nose/Throat: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist. No oral ulcers or mucositis. Pupils equal, round, reactive to light. No scleral icterus.
Lymph nodes: No cervical, supraclavicular, axillary, or inguinal lymphadenopathy detected.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No peripheral edema.
Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No dullness to percussion.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly or splenomegaly. No palpable masses. Midline surgical scar well-healed. No evidence of ascites on exam (no shifting dullness, negative fluid wave).
Extremities: No edema. No petechiae or ecchymoses. Capillary refill brisk bilaterally.
Neurologic: Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Motor strength 5/5 in bilateral upper and lower extremities. Gait normal, heel-to-toe intact. No foot drop. Light touch sensation mildly diminished in distal toes bilaterally. Romberg negative. Fine motor tasks (buttoning, pen grip) normal.
Skin: Full scalp hair regrowth, normal density. No rash.
Pelvic exam: Deferred at patient's preference (post-hysterectomy, post-BSO; vaginal vault exam not performed at this visit given asymptomatic status and negative recent surveillance imaging).

---

LABORATORY RESULTS
CBC (today):
  - WBC: 5.8 x 10^3/uL (normal)
  - ANC: 2,100/uL (normal)
  - Hemoglobin: 11.1 g/dL (Grade 1 anemia; stable, unchanged from prior)
  - MCV: 87 fL (normocytic)
  - Platelets: 119,000/uL (Grade 1 thrombocytopenia; stable, within expected range on niraparib 150 mg)
  - No morphologic abnormalities on differential; no hypersegmented neutrophils, no blasts reported.

Comprehensive metabolic panel:
  - Sodium 139 mEq/L, Potassium 4.0 mEq/L, Bicarbonate 24 mEq/L, BUN 14 mg/dL
  - Creatinine 0.83 mg/dL (stable renal function; no carboplatin-related nephrotoxicity)
  - Total bilirubin 0.6 mg/dL, AST 22 U/L, ALT 18 U/L, Alkaline phosphatase 72 U/L
  - Albumin 4.0 g/dL

Tumor markers:
  - CA-125: 11 U/mL (normal; stable from 12 U/mL at last visit, and 14 U/mL six months prior; continuous normalization maintained)

---

IMAGING
No new imaging performed at today's visit. Most recent CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast (21 months post-diagnosis, 6 months ago): no evidence of peritoneal implants, ascites, lymphadenopathy, or suspicious lesions. Post-surgical pelvic changes stable. Next surveillance imaging planned in approximately 3-6 months per clinical judgment.

Breast MRI approximately 2 months ago per BRCA1 surveillance protocol: negative, no suspicious lesions identified bilaterally. Mammography current and negative.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. HIGH-GRADE SEROUS OVARIAN CARCINOMA, FIGO stage IIIC (pT3c pN1 M0), germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (5382insC), HRD-positive -- CURRENTLY IN COMPLETE REMISSION
   - Patient remains in durable complete remission at 27 months from diagnosis. CA-125 stable at 11 U/mL, well within normal range. No clinical, biochemical, or radiographic evidence of disease recurrence at this time.
   - Continue niraparib (Zejula) 150 mg orally once daily as first-line maintenance therapy. This dose has been well-tolerated since the reduction from 200 mg; stable low-grade cytopenias without further dose-limiting toxicity. Duration of maintenance: continue until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity, consistent with current guidelines and PRIMA/SOLO-1 data. No change to current regimen.
   - Next CA-125 in 3 months.
   - Next surveillance CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast in approximately 6 months. Will consider earlier imaging if new symptoms develop.

2. GRADE 1 THROMBOCYTOPENIA (niraparib-related)
   - Platelets 119,000/uL today, stable over many months at this level. No symptoms. No petechiae, bruising, or bleeding. No further dose reduction required at this time.
   - Continue monthly CBC monitoring while on niraparib.
   - If platelets decline below 75,000/uL again, will hold and reassess; further dose reduction to 100 mg daily would be considered, though this is the lowest standard dose.
   - No ESA indicated at this time for stable Grade 1 anemia (Hgb 11.1 g/dL).

3. RESIDUAL PERIPHERAL NEUROPATHY (paclitaxel-related, Grade 1 residual)
   - Substantially improved from prior nadir of Grade 3 with left foot drop. Now Grade 1 only -- mild bilateral distal toe paresthesias with prolonged standing, not functionally limiting. Foot drop fully resolved. Patient not requesting further intervention.
   - No additional neurology follow-up needed at this time unless symptoms worsen.
   - Continued physical activity encouraged.

4. MENOPAUSE MANAGEMENT (surgical/treatment-induced)
   - Venlafaxine 37.5 mg daily for vasomotor symptoms -- adequate effect per patient.
   - Vaginal lubricants for genitourinary symptoms of menopause -- adequate.
   - Hormone replacement therapy remains contraindicated given history of ER-positive HGSC.
   - Bone health: counsel regarding calcium (1200 mg/day) and vitamin D (1000-2000 IU/day) supplementation for bone health in postmenopausal state. Baseline DEXA scan considered -- will order.

5. BRCA1 GERMLINE CARRIER MANAGEMENT
   - Bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy completed as part of cytoreductive surgery -- ovarian/fallopian tube cancer risk reduction accomplished.
   - Breast surveillance: annual mammography and breast MRI per NCCN guidelines for BRCA1 carriers -- up to date, most recent MRI negative.
   - Risk-reducing bilateral mastectomy discussed again. Patient continues to decline surgical prophylaxis and prefers ongoing imaging surveillance. This preference is documented and respected. She is well-informed of residual lifetime breast cancer risk.
   - BRCA1 survivor registry study: patient enrolled and engaged.
   - Family cascade testing: patient's daughter confirmed BRCA1 positive (5382insC) and enrolled in high-risk program. Son's testing status unknown; patient advised to encourage testing.

6. PSYCHOSOCIAL WELLBEING
   - Mild anxiety related to cancer recurrence is acknowledged and being addressed via ovarian cancer survivors' support group attendance. Functioning well, returned to full-time work.
   - No escalation of psychiatric care needed at this time. If anxiety becomes more impairing, referral for individual therapy or psychiatry consultation is available.

Return to clinic: 3 months. Earlier if concerns arise.

Total time: 45 minutes (>50% counseling and coordination of care).

Electronically signed by Attending Gynecologic Oncologist.
